---
title: "Mexican Primrose-Willow (Ludwigia octovalvis)"
slug: "ludwigia-octovalvis"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/257754412/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Mexican Primrose-Willow"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Myrtales"
tax_family: "Onagraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Myrtales", "Onagraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/257754412/medium.jpg" alt="Mexican Primrose-Willow" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/robmcirving">
robmcirving</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/1495691639">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Myrtales
-   **Family:** Onagraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=164861)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as ditches, pond margins, and marshes.

## Natural history notes

An upright wetland plant characterized by its narrow leaves and bright yellow, four-petaled flowers. In Florida, it is commonly found in moist to wet habitats such as ditches, pond margins, and marshes, often in disturbed areas. It grows during the warmer months and can form dense stands in shallow water or seasonally flooded sites.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/164861-Ludwigia-octovalvis)
-   [Local profile](https://toptropicals.com/catalog/uid/ludwigia_octovalvis.htm?srsltid=AfmBOoq16UqXgUyNGU5nTURvojBN-A-fp0KDZlBTOysTePOx0taNAjQg)
